title: grewia voloina
intro: Grewia voloina, also known as the Madagascar hawthorn, is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Grewia voloina include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce the risk of heart disease, and alleviate symptoms of fever and digestive issues. Grewia voloina can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
